dna.description: OVHcloud is a leading European cloud infrastructure provider offering a comprehensive range of cloud computing services including public cloud, private cloud, bare metal servers, web hosting, and managed Kubernetes. As Europe's largest cloud provider and a champion of digital sovereignty, OVHcloud operates a global network of data centers with a vertically integrated model, manufacturing its own servers. Listed on Euronext Paris (OVH), OVHcloud serves over 1.6 million customers worldwide.
dna.sub_industry: Cloud Infrastructure / IaaS / PaaS / Web Hosting
dna.business_model: B2B IaaS/PaaS Subscription + Pay-as-you-go Consumption + Managed Services
products: Public Cloud (OpenStack-based IaaS with compute, storage, networking)
Hosted Private Cloud powered by VMware
Bare Metal Cloud (dedicated servers on demand)
Web Cloud (web hosting, domain names, email)
AI Training and AI Deploy (GPU cloud for machine learning)
Network and Security (Anti-DDoS, Load Balancer, vRack)
features: Predictable flat-rate pricing with no egress fees
Anti-DDoS protection included on all services
Water-cooled server technology for energy efficiency
target_segments: Financial services firms with data sovereignty requirements
use_cases: Hosting scalable web applications and SaaS platforms
AI model training on GPU clusters
Sovereign cloud for regulated data workloads
Big data analytics and data lake storage
Game server hosting and streaming infrastructure
dna.pricing_model: Pay-as-you-go hourly or monthly billing for cloud resources. Flat-rate pricing for dedicated servers and hosting. No egress fees on most services. Volume discounts and committed-use contracts for enterprise accounts.
dna.company_size_signals: 2,500+ employees, 1.6 million+ customers, revenue of EUR 870 million (2024). Headquartered in Roubaix, France. Listed on Euronext Paris (OVH). Operates 40+ data centers across 4 continents.
dna.tone_of_voice: Transparent, sovereignty-focused, technically grounded, with emphasis on European values, open-source, and environmental responsibility

market.market_size_estimate: Global cloud infrastructure market (IaaS + PaaS): ~USD 300 billion (2024). European cloud market: ~USD 75 billion. Web hosting market: ~USD 100 billion. Total addressable market for OVHcloud: ~USD 60 billion.
market.growth_trend: Growing at 18-22% CAGR globally, with European sovereign cloud segment growing faster at 25-30% driven by regulatory requirements (GDPR, NIS2, DORA). GPU cloud for AI training growing at 40%+ CAGR. OVHcloud growing at 10-12% with acceleration expected from sovereign cloud mandates.
likely_competitors: AWS (dominant global cloud provider with broadest service portfolio)
Microsoft Azure (strong enterprise presence with Office 365 integration)
Google Cloud Platform (leading in AI/ML and data analytics)
Hetzner (German low-cost dedicated and cloud server provider)
Scaleway (French cloud provider, subsidiary of Iliad Group)
Ionos (1&1, German hosting and cloud provider)
DigitalOcean (developer-focused cloud for SMBs and startups)
competitive_advantages: Only major European cloud provider with vertically integrated manufacturing
SecNumCloud certification for highest sovereignty requirements
No egress fees providing predictable total cost of ownership
40+ own data centers with proprietary water-cooling technology
Price-performance leadership in bare metal and GPU compute
market_challenges: US hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) dominate with 65%+ market share
Limited enterprise sales force compared to hyperscalers
Perception gap: seen as hosting company rather than enterprise cloud
Strasbourg data center fire in 2021 impacted trust
Smaller ecosystem of managed services and marketplace offerings
customer_pain_points: Unpredictable cloud bills due to egress fees and hidden costs
Data sovereignty concerns with US-based cloud providers (CLOUD Act)
Vendor lock-in to proprietary hyperscaler services
Lack of GDPR-compliant cloud infrastructure options
Need for sovereign cloud to meet regulatory requirements (NIS2, DORA)
buying_triggers: Regulatory requirement for European data residency
Unexpected cloud cost overrun with current hyperscaler
Need for SecNumCloud or HDS certified infrastructure
Launch of AI/ML initiative requiring affordable GPU compute
Cloud repatriation strategy to reduce hyperscaler dependency
New data sovereignty regulation (NIS2, DORA) taking effect
Startup scaling beyond shared hosting to cloud infrastructure
market.sales_cycle_estimate: 1-2 weeks for self-service web hosting and cloud, 1-3 months for mid-market cloud migration projects, 3-9 months for enterprise sovereign cloud and private cloud deployments
ideal_customer_characteristics: European company with data sovereignty or GDPR compliance requirements
Annual cloud infrastructure spend between USD 50,000 and USD 5 million
Technology team comfortable with OpenStack and Kubernetes
Organization seeking predictable cloud costs without egress fees
Company in regulated industry (healthcare, finance, public sector)
SaaS provider needing scalable European-hosted infrastructure
Business looking to reduce dependency on US hyperscalers
Organization with bare metal or high-performance compute needs
AI/ML team needing affordable GPU cloud resources
Company valuing open-source and vendor-neutral cloud platforms
anti_icp_signals: Enterprise deeply embedded in AWS or Azure ecosystem with no migration appetite
Company requiring 200+ managed cloud services (database, analytics, IoT)
Organization with no European operations or data residency needs
Business needing global edge locations in 50+ countries
Startup wanting fully managed serverless-only architecture
Company with no technical team capable of managing cloud infrastructure
Organization requiring FedRAMP or US government certifications
expansion_opportunities: Sovereign cloud for European public sector and defense
AI/ML GPU cloud competing with hyperscaler pricing
Managed database and analytics services expansion
industry_trends: European digital sovereignty becoming regulatory mandate
Cloud repatriation trend as enterprises optimize costs
AI infrastructure demand exploding for training and inference
Multi-cloud and hybrid cloud as default enterprise strategy
Sustainability and energy efficiency as cloud selection criteria
Open-source cloud stacks gaining enterprise adoption
